title: hieracium solidagineum subsp. flexiflorum
intro: Hieracium solidagineum subsp. flexiflorum, a lesser-known subspecies of the hawkweed plant, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
